posts.name: Charlie Carnes
posts.cooked:
• 2019 Beau Rivage Chenin Blanc - USA, California, Central Valley, Clarksburg (10/18/2021)
    This was a gift from Alfert. Man this is amazing. It is fresh and crisp, with light bees wax, sweet straw, super light lanolin and lemon, pretty and easygoing mineral trait. Only a Chenin Blanc has a swallow like this, which is to say, it is light, bright, but with a cool dead blow hammer effect, which neither dulls or warms, just spreads out. Loving my first bottle of this!!! (94 pts.)

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: S teve R edenbaugh
posts.cooked: Nice note…I think I’m on my fourth 6-pack and I tend to drink them in bunches. This is a drop-dead gift from Bacchus when you compare quality and price.

posts.name: Brian Pinci
posts.cooked: I purchased some on Berserker day. Quite good. I’ll definitely get more when I run out.

posts.name: Andy Ma
posts.cooked: What are some good pairings for this?

posts.name: Charlie Carnes
posts.cooked:
What are some good pairings for this?

I drank mine with homemade Cuban sandwiches. But this will go with just about anything, or all by its lonesome!

IT WAS A GIFT TO ME, CARNES! ME! I knew it was your treacherous ass who stole it from me!

I was tricked, I tell you, tricked! If I find another bottle in highlands I’ll put it in the communal cellar!!!

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
What are some good pairings for this?

I’m into my 4th case of this wine, honestly the better question is, “what doesn’t it pair well with”. Now bear in mind, I also just happen to love Chenin Blanc. My favorite is with pan seared crab cakes. But I have pretty much had it with everything but red meat.

posts.name: Blair Ridley
posts.cooked: How’d you guys get the 2019? I see only the 2018 on the site (which I still need to buy).

posts.name: Vince T s 3 n g
posts.cooked:
How’d you guys get the 2019? I see only the 2018 on the site (which I still need to buy).

BerserkerDay XI had 6 packs - 2 each of the 17, 18 and 19.

posts.name: Jud Reis
posts.cooked: Popped and poured at a bit cooler than cellar temp. Lime, a soapy note( in a good way if that makes any sense), pepper and white flowers on the nose. Really lovely rich texture with sweetish citrus fruit - lime and grapefruit on the attack and a nice long acid filled finish. Still a youngster and I think this will benefit from a few more years in the cellar.
